canonical_question: What are the common backtracking patterns?
constraints: Worst-case exponential time O(b^d); always estimate feasibility before implementing
State must be fully restored after each recursive call; partial undo causes silent corruption
Pruning is not optional for production use; unpruned backtracking is computationally infeasible
Base case must terminate recursion; missing base case causes infinite recursion / stack overflow
Mutable shared state must be passed by reference and restored, not copied per call
